perturbation.original_text: 1.9 "Security Measures" shall mean those procedures and precautions described in Exhibit A, for maintaining the security of the Product and Usage Data required under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 "Security Measures" shall mean general guidelines for maintaining the security of the Product and Usage Data required under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Security Measures' references Exhibit A for specific procedures and precautions. By changing it to 'general guidelines,' it removes the specific details, creating ambiguity and contradiction in later sections that mandate strict adherence to the undefined Security Measures. This creates an in-text contradiction, where the contract requires adherence to measures that are no longer clearly defined. In section 2.5 there is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5 Inktomi shall strictly adhere to all Security Measures in performing the Services, including without limitation securing the Usage Data, which it may possess or have under its control from time to time, from unauthorized access and modification.
perturbation.justification: The change from defining 'Security Measures' as specific procedures in Exhibit A to 'general guidelines' directly contradicts section 2.5. Section 2.5 requires strict adherence to all 'Security Measures'. By removing the specific procedures, the contract now mandates adherence to something vaguely defined, creating a contradiction. This fits the definition of an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language (the reference to Exhibit A) was removed, creating a contradiction with the obligation to strictly adhere to these now undefined measures in Section 2.5.

perturbation.original_text: 2.7 Inktomi will assign two (2) full-time Inktomi employees exclusively dedicated to maintenance duties hereunder. Such employees are identified in Exhibit D attached hereto; and their replacements shall be subject to Microsoft's prior written approval (which approval Microsoft will not unreasonably withhold). Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Microsoft and Inktomi mutually agree in writing, additional Inktomi employees may be required to be assigned to maintenance duties hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.7 Inktomi will assign employees to maintenance duties hereunder. Additional employees may be assigned to maintenance duties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies two full-time employees and references Exhibit D for their identities, along with a process for replacements subject to Microsoft's approval. Removing the specific number of employees and the reference to Exhibit D introduces ambiguity. This omission creates an in-text contradiction in section 4.1(f), where payment is calculated based on a specific number of employees, which is no longer clearly defined. The payment structure depends on a defined number of employees which are no longer defined.

Notwithstanding section 4.2 below, such payments shall be due in immediately available funds on the first business day of each month. The parties acknowledge that the monthly fee under this clause (b) will increase throughout the Term if and to the extent that Microsoft's Hosting Server requirements increase, but, notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, no amounts shall be payable under this clause (b) attributable to any Hosting Server which is more than [*] years old; (c) an amount equal to the [*] and [*] of the new Hosting Servers purchased by Inktomi pursuant to Section 3.1 above ([*] any amounts paid by Inktomi to Exodus for such services or attributable to the employees referred to in clause (f) below), payable if and when Inktomi pays such maintenance costs; Inktomi will use its commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the annual hardware and software maintenance costs for each such new Hosting Server are not more than [*] percent ([*]%) of the purchase price of such New Hosting Server, and Microsoft will not be obligated to pay higher maintenance costs than such [*]% annual estimate without its prior written consent; (d) an amount equal to [*] Percent ([*]%) of the sum of the amounts payable under clauses (b) and (c) above (attributable as Inktomi's management fee for providing the Services); (e) an amount equal to Microsoft's [*] of the [*] [*] by Inktomi to Exodus in connection with the applicable Hosting Servers cluster, computed in accordance with Exhibit [*], [*] the [*] new Hosting Servers are [*] at Exodus; and (f) an amount equal to [*] per month per person identified in Section 2.7 above, [*] of the new Hosting Servers. perturbation.justification: The original text specified that Inktomi would assign two full-time employees to maintenance duties, with their identities listed in Exhibit D. This is directly tied to Section 4.1(f), which calculates payment based on a per-person amount for the employees identified in Section 2.7. The changed text removes the specification of 'two full-time employees' and the reference to Exhibit D. This omission creates a contradiction, because section 4.1(f) refers back to section 2.7 for an exact number of people and the changed 2.7 no longer specifies any number, making it impossible to determine the payment amount as described by section 4.1(f).

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Microsoft acknowledges that, pursuant to Inktomi's contractual arrangement with its subcontractor, Exodus Communications, Inc. ("Exodus"), Inktomi will locate the Hosting Servers at the facilities of Exodus, and Exodus will provide power and Internet telecommunications services to the Hosting Servers. However, Microsoft will have no obligations or liabilities to Exodus, Inktomi will remain liable for providing all 6 Services to Microsoft notwithstanding its arrangement with Exodus, and Inktomi will [*] and [*] against from any and all [*] to [*] (in accordance with the procedures specified in Section [*] below). A copy of the contract(s) between Inktomi and Exodus is/are attached hereto as Exhibit [*], and Inktomi shall not modify said contract(s) or replace Exodus as its subcontractor for the applicable services (including without limitation by having Inktomi perform the Services directly) without Microsoft's prior written approval (which approval Microsoft agrees to not unreasonably withhold). Inktomi shall provide Exodus with a copy of the Security Measures applicable under this Agreement and will use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that Exodus strictly adheres to all such Security Measures.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Inktomi will locate the Hosting Servers at a suitable facility. Inktomi will remain liable for providing all 6 Services to Microsoft. Inktomi shall not replace its subcontractor for the applicable services without Microsoft's prior written approval. Inktomi will use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the subcontractor strictly adheres to all Security Measures.
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the specific reference to 'Exodus Communications, Inc.' as the subcontractor and replaces it with a general term, 'a suitable facility'. This omission creates ambiguity, especially in section 4.1 (e), where payments are tied to costs incurred with 'Exodus'. Removing the specific reference and removing 'Exhibit C' creates an in-text contradiction, as the agreement now refers to costs related to a subcontractor that is not specifically identified.

Therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.
